template_name: US Copyright Office Part 3: Generative AI and Copyright Training
source_legislation: US Copyright Office, Copyright and Artificial Intelligence Part 3: Generative AI Training (2025)

provisions.normative_text_ref: USCO Part 3 — Lawful Acquisition of Training Data
provisions.description: Generative AI providers should document the lawful acquisition of training data, including source identification, acquisition method (licensed, fair use, public domain), and legal basis for each data source

sub_provisions.description: Each training data source should be identified with a URI, acquisition date, and legal basis
Documentation should establish a clear chain of custody from original source to training pipeline

evaluation.message: At least one data_provenance record is required — the US Copyright Office Part 3 guidance expects documented provenance of training data including source identification and acquisition method

Every data_provenance record must specify acquisition_method (licensed, scraped, public_domain, synthetic, fair_use) — the Copyright Office guidance requires clear documentation of how training data was obtained
Data provenance records should include source_uri to identify the origin of training data for legal defensibility
Data provenance records should be linked to dataset entities for traceability of acquisition records to specific training datasets

provisions.description: Where training data is obtained through licensing, providers should document the licensor, scope, restrictions, content type, and expiration of each licensing agreement

evaluation.message: At least one license_record is required — the US Copyright Office Part 3 guidance expects documentation of licensing agreements for copyrighted training data
License records should specify the licensor to establish the granting party for training data licensing per Copyright Office guidance
License records should specify the scope of the licensing agreement to verify coverage of intended training uses

provisions.normative_text_ref: USCO Part 3 — Curation and Filtering Processes
provisions.description: Providers should document their data curation and filtering processes including deduplication methods, content removal decisions, and filtering criteria applied to training data

evaluation.message: At least one data_provenance record is required — the US Copyright Office Part 3 guidance expects documentation of data curation and filtering processes
Every data_provenance record must specify processing_steps — the Copyright Office guidance requires documentation of curation, filtering, and deduplication methods applied to training data

provisions.normative_text_ref: USCO Part 3 — Memorization and Regurgitation Risk
provisions.description: Providers should document measures taken to detect and mitigate memorization and regurgitation risks, including detection methods, test results, and mitigation techniques applied

evaluation.message: At least one evaluation_report record is required — the US Copyright Office Part 3 guidance expects documented evidence of memorization/regurgitation risk detection and mitigation
Evaluation reports should document the methodology used for memorization detection and mitigation assessment

provisions.normative_text_ref: USCO Part 3 — Technical Guardrails Against Infringing Output
provisions.description: Providers should document technical guardrails implemented to prevent the generation of output that infringes copyright, including prompt filtering, content matching, and blocking mechanisms

evaluation.message: At least one evaluation_report record is required — the US Copyright Office Part 3 guidance expects documented evidence of output guardrails including effectiveness testing of anti-infringement measures
A copyright_rights_reservation record is recommended to document TDM opt-out compliance and rights reservation signal handling as part of the output guardrails framework
